title: Mubadala invests $75m in WHOOP to expand AI-driven preventative healthcare in the UAE - Arabian Business:
summary: Mubadala will invest $75m in WHOOP as the performance technology company expands research, biomarker testing and digital health services in the UAE
thbAction: Map the news to THB's provider CRM for referral growth, patient lifecycle engagement, and multi-facility relationship management across hospital and provider networks.
contentHtml:
Mubadala Investment Company has announced a strategic partnership with human performance technology company WHOOP, anchored by a $75m investment aimed at expanding preventative healthcare, performance science and advanced health research capabilities in the UAE.

The partnership, unveiled during Make it in the Emirates 2026, supports broader UAE ambitions to strengthen advanced healthcare ecosystems, attract global innovation and position Abu Dhabi as a hub for next-generation life sciences and digital health technologies.

As part of the agreement, WHOOP will establish a dedicated UAE office, expand its Advanced Labs offering and launch a new research initiative focused on cardiovascular health, sleep science and behavioural performance.

The partnership reflects Mubadala’s broader strategy to expand investment across life sciences and healthcare, particularly in proactive and data-enabled health systems.

According to the announcement, the collaboration will integrate performance science, longitudinal research, biomarker testing and localised digital health access to strengthen the UAE’s healthcare value chain across diagnostics, genomics, prevention and behavioural optimisation.

WHOOP expands UAE health operations

WHOOP will also introduce full Arabic localisation across its digital platform as part of the UAE expansion.

The company said the UAE will become the first international market outside the United States to replicate WHOOP’s full biomarker experience through WHOOP Advanced Labs.

The initiative is designed to support data-driven preventative healthcare models at scale and strengthen precision diagnostics capabilities within the national healthcare ecosystem.

The companies also announced the launch of the WHOOP–Mubadala Health Research Initiative.

The programme will support structured collaboration with leading UAE institutions to conduct longitudinal studies in cardiovascular health, recovery, sleep science and behavioural performance.

UAE research initiative launched

The initiative is intended to strengthen advanced research capabilities and support discoveries in performance science, while reinforcing the UAE’s growing position in preventative healthcare and longevity research.

Will Ahmed, Founder and CEO of WHOOP, said: “Our journey in the UAE began in 2017, when we partnered with Expo 2020 Dubai to support worker wellness at scale. Since then, we have seen strong engagement and meaningful growth across the region.

“Our partnership with Mubadala reflects a shared ambition to build one of the world’s most advanced ecosystems for preventative health, performance science, and research-led innovation.”

Ali Eid AlMheiri, Executive Director, Diversified Assets, UAE Investments Platform at Mubadala, said: “Attracting leading global companies to establish and grow in Abu Dhabi is central to how we build future-focused sectors and strengthen the UAE’s economic competitiveness.

“Through our partnership with WHOOP, we are bringing advanced health technology, research capabilities, and international expertise into the local ecosystem. This supports long-term knowledge creation, enables innovation, and helps develop industries that contribute to health resilience, talent development, and economic diversification.”

The announcement highlights the increasing overlap between industrial policy, healthcare innovation and economic diversification within the UAE’s long-term strategy.

WHOOP first established a presence in the UAE in 2017 through its involvement in Expo 2020 Dubai’s Worker Wellness Program. The latest partnership expands that relationship into a broader research-led health and performance platform aligned with the UAE’s innovation and healthcare priorities.
